vulnerable_assignments.title: Weekly Discussion Post (Week 3)
vulnerabilities: Requests a 'discussion post', a highly standard format in LLM training data.
Low constraint density. The prompt allows for generic comparisons of Bentham and Kant without local tethering.
Zero relational markers. The 'reply to two classmates' requirement is structurally rote and easily automated.

vulnerable_assignments.title: Midterm Essay (Week 8)
vulnerabilities: Requests a '5-paragraph essay' on the 'Trolley Problem', which is one of the most widely modeled ethical dilemmas in generative AI.
Requires APA format and 3 peer-reviewed sources. LLMs are highly proficient at hallucinating or formatting these structures if not strictly constrained by recent/local databases.

vulnerable_assignments.title: Final Research Paper (Week 15)
vulnerabilities: Requests a '10-page research paper'. Volume of text is not a barrier to generative AI.
Topic (Technology and Privacy) is extremely broad and highly represented in training data.
High concentration of lower-order structural requirements (introduction, literature review) that are easily templated by AI.

agent_recommendation: The entire structural assessment of this course relies on unverified, text-based products (essays, discussion boards, research papers). The Designer must pivot these to process-oriented, high-constraint multimodal deliverables.
